metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rationale explaining why the code-review novelty floor needs no activation flag analogous to `novelty_drop_active`, OR add an activation config key (e.g. `code_review_novelty_drop_active`, default `False`) so the floor can be validated on a calibration corpus before going live.
findings.finding: The plan drops the activation flag (`novelty_drop_active`) without providing a rationale that covers the post-first-run case. Plan-review requires `novelty_drop_active` because once prior memory exists, the floor is live for every subsequent run — and the code-review novelty discriminator has not been evaluated for the code-review domain before this story ships. The plan's stated self-gate ('inert whenever no prior memory') is only true for a session's very first review; any subsequent review in the same session (or change) has prior memory and the floor activates immediately, with no eval evidence that the code-review novelty discriminator meets the same bar `discriminates_novelty` set for plan-review.
findings.location: What section: 'Thresholds/activation (decided)' / AC line 5: 'Introduce NO new config key or activation flag'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why the code-review novelty floor can safely omit the `novelty_drop_active` evidence gate that plan-review requires, or add an equivalent eval-clearance requirement (e.g. a `code_review_novelty_drop_active` flag defaulting False, flipped only after a `discriminates_novelty`-equivalent eval clears its bar for the line-region scoring domain).
findings.finding: The plan drops the `novelty_drop_active` evidence gate (present in plan-review's triple-gated floor) without justification, substituting only a data-driven 'no prior memory → inert' self-gate. The plan-review floor required an eval (`discriminates_novelty`) to clear its bar before the flag was flippable; the code-review floor introduces a new scoring domain (line-region `cites_prior_location` semantics, file-level SHA comparison) that has not been validated by an analogous eval. Shipping a live-dropping floor without an eval gate is a novel architectural deviation from the established prior art, and the plan offers no rationale for why the evidence gate is unnecessary in the code-review context.
findings.location: ## What — 'Thresholds/activation (decided)' bullet; AC item 5 ('no new config key or activation flag')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `code_review_novelty_drop_active: bool = False` flag to the `verify` config section (mirroring `novelty_drop_active`) and gate `region_gated_floor` on it. This makes the floor inert by default and gives operators the same eval-clearance pathway. Document the eval bar in the AC ('flip only after a `discriminates_novelty`-equivalent eval validates line-region scoring for code review').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact mechanism for coaching omission: (a) name the field (`carried_from`) on the finding dict and confirm it is an extra key passed through as-is (not in the Pydantic schema), AND (b) specify which step reads it to suppress coaching (e.g. `code_review_coach_inputs` filters findings where `carried_from` is non-empty from the coach listing, while still including them in `surfaced`).
findings.finding: The plan stamps carryover findings with `carried_from = matched_prior_id` and says coaching is "OMITTED" for them, but never specifies where `carried_from` lives in the data model or how the coaching step skips it. The `CodeFinding` Pydantic model (confirmed in `code_review/contracts.py:34-58`) has no `carried_from` field. The `code_review_coach_inputs` step (workflow_ops.py:465-474) reads `surfaced` findings and passes all of them to `review_kernel.coach_listing` — there is no filter hook. The plan does not say: (a) whether `carried_from` is an extra dict key on the finding (outside the schema), (b) whether `coach_inputs` is modified to skip findings with `carried_from` set, or (c) whether the verdict assembly step strips coaching for those findings post-hoc.
findings.location: What / NEW `region_gated_floor` / Carryover labeling section; AC line 6

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one end-to-end proving command that exercises the live `code-novelty` reviewer path (e.g., `rebar review --code <sample-diff>` against a real LLM endpoint and asserting a `drop_reason=novelty-region` annotation in the output), and add grep/pytest commands for ACs 1–8 (e.g., `grep -r 'REGION_UNCHANGED' src/rebar/llm/code_review/region_gate.py`, `python -c 'from rebar.llm.code_review.contracts import register_contracts; register_contracts()'`).
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is described for ACs 1–8 (file existence, contract registration, constant definitions, drop logic, threshold reuse, carryover stamping, fail-safe, neutrality), and the new always-on region_gated_floor code path — which calls the live `code-novelty` LLM reviewer — has no acceptance criterion that exercises it end-to-end against the live model. All test coverage described (AC 9) uses a mocked reader, meaning the live LLM boundary and the wiring in gate_dispatch.py are never exercised by any stated proving command.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria (all bullets) / ## Scope (gate_dispatch.py wiring) / ## What (code-novelty reviewer id)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC covering the `gate_dispatch.py` wiring: e.g. 'The floor is invoked from `gate_dispatch.py` before the sidecar emit, confirmed by an integration test or a unit test mocking the dispatch path.'
findings.finding: The Scope section lists `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` as a required modification ('invoke the floor before emit'), but no acceptance criterion covers this file or its integration work — it is an orphaned deliverable with no verifiable AC.
findings.location: Scope section / AC set (all 9 ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`gate_dispatch.py` invokes `region_gated_floor` in the produce path before the sidecar emit; a test (mocked reader + dispatcher) confirms that findings passing through gate_dispatch are subject to the floor.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one integration / end-to-end acceptance criterion that exercises the actual `code-novelty` RunRequest (with real or realistic contract resolution) and the gate_dispatch.py wiring in a live-ish produce path, so the full novelty sub-call → region gate → drop decision chain is verified beyond mocked units.
findings.finding: The test plan covers only mocked-reader unit tests; the LLM novelty sub-call (`RunRequest(reviewers=["code-novelty"])`), contract registration (`contracts.register_contracts()`), and gate_dispatch.py wiring are never exercised end-to-end. Since `region_gated_floor` is being wired into the default produce path, the offline-only test suite can go green while the actual live LLM boundary (wrong prompt schema, missing contract registration, broken gate_dispatch wiring) is silently broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — final bullet ('Tests (mocked reader)') and Scope — gate_dispatch.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line such as: 'Integration smoke test: a synthetic two-pass code review (second pass with a prior memory record) correctly invokes the `code-novelty` sub-call with a registered contract, and the gate_dispatch wiring drops a qualifying unchanged-region finding from the emitted sidecar payload (verified against the real produce path, not mocked scores).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document an explicit rollback/back-out path for the floor behavior change (e.g. 'clear the prior-findings store for affected keys', 'revert contracts registration', or 'set novelty_drop_threshold to 0.0 to disable dropping') so operators have a reversibility mechanism without a full code rollback.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly rejects all rollback mechanisms (no feature flag, no activation toggle) while changing existing surfacing behavior (findings previously always surfaced can now be dropped) for any session/change key that already has prior memory. The 'self-gates on no prior memory' argument only covers brand-new reviews; for existing reviews with prior memory, the behavior change is immediate and irreversible on deployment with no documented back-out path.
findings.location: What section — Thresholds/activation paragraph; overall plan
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'To disable the floor without a code rollback, set `verify.novelty_drop_threshold` to a value ≥ 1.0 (effectively disabling all drops) via the existing kernel config — no new key needed.' This uses the existing threshold axis as an emergency shutoff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rationale in the plan for why `verify.novelty_drop_active` is not needed for code-review's `region_gated_floor` (e.g. 'the REGION_UNCHANGED guard is the structural safety net that plan-review's flag provides'), so the design intent is clear and reviewers can validate it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the floor SELF-GATES: it is inert whenever there is no prior memory … so it needs no flag' and 'NO eval-gated activation flag', but the plan-review analog (`_maybe_apply_rising_floor`) actually requires a THIRD gate: `verify.novelty_drop_active` (confirmed at plan_review/__init__.py:203). The plan does not explain why the code-review floor is safe without this guard, making the safety argument unverifiable from the plan text alone.
findings.location: What section — 'Thresholds/activation (decided): … Introduce NO new config key and NO eval-gated activation flag'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line (and a corresponding test or integration smoke-check) that verifies `gate_dispatch.py` correctly invokes `region_gated_floor` before the sidecar emit, so the dispatch wiring has an end-state-observable proof.
findings.finding: `gate_dispatch.py` is listed in Scope as the invocation site for `region_gated_floor` but its role is not described in the What section and no Acceptance Criterion covers the wiring there, leaving the integration point under-specified.
findings.location: Scope: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py (invoke the floor before emit)`; AC section (no corresponding AC entry)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: '`gate_dispatch.py` calls `region_gated_floor` on the decided advisory set before emit; a unit/integration test confirms that a finding on an unchanged region that would be dropped is absent from the emitted payload when dispatched through `gate_dispatch`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `latest_code_review_result(key)` from revenued-thickset-dassie is merged and callable before this story is implemented, OR explicitly declare the story blocked on it and provide an import-guard / lazy-import pattern so unit tests can run against a mock without the real function present.
findings.finding: `latest_code_review_result(key)` is called as a ready-to-consume primitive in the 'Consume the reader' section, but the function does not exist in the codebase — it is the deliverable of the dependency ticket revenued-thickset-dassie, which is currently open/unimplemented.
findings.location: What section — 'Consume the reader. Call `latest_code_review_result(key)` (revenued-thickset-dassie)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope or Dependencies section, mark this story as 'blocked on revenued-thickset-dassie (open)' and specify that `region_gated_floor` should import the reader lazily (inside the function body) so the module loads without the reader present and tests can inject a mock.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `revenued-thickset-dassie` as an explicit dependency (the reader story) in this plan's `Depends on` section, or note the strict ordering constraint, so the implementation cannot proceed without the reader being available.
findings.finding: `latest_code_review_result(key)` does not yet exist in the codebase — it is the deliverable of the still-OPEN dependency story `revenued-thickset-dassie`. The plan treats it as an available callable but it is absent from `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` today.
findings.location: What section — 'Consume the reader. Call `latest_code_review_result(key)`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `revenued-thickset-dassie` to the plan's `Scope > Depends on` block (it already names the ticket in prose but does not declare it as a blocking dep).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism for omitting coaching from carryover findings — e.g. 'exclude findings with `carried_from` set from the `code_review_coach_inputs` surfaced list before the Pass-4 call' — so the AC is unambiguously testable.
findings.finding: The mechanism for omitting coaching for carryover findings is unspecified. The plan says a carryover (low-novelty, non-dropped) finding 'has its coaching OMITTED, while remaining surfaced', but the existing code-review coach path generates coaching from the full `surfaced` list in `code_review_coach_inputs` and `code_review_coach`. The plan gives no concrete mechanism: should carried findings be filtered out of the coaching inputs list before the Pass-4 call, or should the sidecar/verdict assembly skip coaching notes that reference a carried finding's id, or something else? Without specifying this, an implementer cannot determine whether the coaching-omission is a pre-coach-call filter, a post-render suppression, or a sidecar-layer concern.
findings.location: What section: 'Carryover labeling' bullet / AC line 6: 'Carryover findings are stamped carried_from = the matched prior finding's id (string) and omit coaching, but remain surfaced.'
findings.suggested_fix: Filter findings with a populated `carried_from` field out of the `surfaced` list passed to `code_review_coach_inputs` (pre-Pass-4 filter), so the coach prompt never receives carryover findings and no post-render suppression is needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `base` argument `region_gate.py`'s detector must pass to `attest._hash_file` (i.e. `attest._hash_basis(repo_root)` — the same basis used when building the `deps` map in the prior review) so the hash comparison is basis-consistent.
findings.finding: `region_gate.py`'s detector is specified to call `attest._hash_file(path, base=?)` to hash the current file, but the plan never specifies what `base` argument to pass. `_hash_file` takes a `base` (repo root) that determines the absolute path for relative `path` values. The prior `deps` map (from the reader) stores hashes taken at the review's attested-snapshot root (per revenued-thickset-dassie: `attest._hash_basis(repo_root)`). If the detector hashes against a different base (e.g. the current working tree vs. the snapshot), every region will spuriously compare as `CHANGED`, defeating the unchanged-region gate entirely.
findings.location: What / NEW `region_gate.py` / Citation basis section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `region_gate.py` spec: 'Hash using `attest._hash_file(path, base=attest._hash_basis(repo_root))` — the same basis the prior review used when storing `deps` — so the comparison is consistent across attested and local runs.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one observable signal (structured log warning + a counter metric) when the fail-safe branch activates, recording the exception type and the review key so operators can detect silent floor degradation.
findings.finding: The fail-safe try/except silently swallows errors with no log, metric, or trace emitted when it activates, making it operationally invisible when the novelty floor is failing and passing everything through.
findings.location: What section — FAIL-SAFE paragraph; AC line 'Any reader/hash/compare/novelty error returns the full unfiltered finding set'
findings.suggested_fix: In the except block, emit `logger.warning('region_gated_floor fail-safe activated for key=%s: %s', key, exc)` and increment a counter metric (e.g. `metrics.increment('code_review.novelty_floor.failsafe')`) before returning the unfiltered set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a latency done-definition and timeout for the novelty sub-call (e.g. inherit the kernel runner's existing deadline, or state an explicit wall-clock bound), and confirm the fail-safe's try/except covers timeout errors so a hanging call degrades gracefully.
findings.finding: The synchronous novelty LLM sub-call is placed on the produce hot-path (before sidecar emit) with no timeout or latency done-definition stated, so a slow or hanging LLM response blocks the entire produce path indefinitely.
findings.location: What section — 'NEW region_gated_floor (in code_review/workflow_ops.py, applied over the decided advisory set in the produce path before the sidecar emit)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or update an ADR (or CLAUDE.md section) capturing the region-gated floor pipeline stage, the code-specific redefinition of `cites_prior_location`, and the new `carried_from`/`drop_reason` payload fields.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (region_gated_floor), a redefined shared-contract field (cites_prior_location), and new payload fields (carried_from, drop_reason) — all architectural decisions — without updating any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc.
findings.location: ## What (cites_prior_location REDEFINED section), ## Scope, Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Create a brief ADR (e.g., ADR 0009 or an addendum to ADR 0008) that records: (1) the region-gated floor as a new code-review pipeline stage, (2) the semantic split of `cites_prior_location` between plan-review and code-review contexts, (3) the `carried_from`/`drop_reason` payload field contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a measurement plan or pilot step (e.g., a shadow-mode run logging would-drop counts without actually dropping) so code-review threshold fitness can be validated before the floor is fully active.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that plan-review calibrated thresholds (`verify.novelty_drop_threshold`, `verify.novelty_priority_floor`) are appropriate for code-review without any measurement or pilot, yet code-review findings have different priority distributions and novelty dynamics than plan-review findings.
findings.location: ## What — Thresholds/activation section: 'reuse the EXISTING kernel thresholds ... one calibration axis shared with plan-review'
findings.suggested_fix: Instrument `region_gated_floor` to emit a structured log entry for every would-drop decision (priority, novelty, region, final outcome) for the first N reviews, enabling post-hoc calibration analysis without blocking the ship.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'novel → dropped' language conflict: if the gate reuses `rising_floor_drop` unchanged, clarify in the Why section and AC-9 test label that the dropped findings are LOW-novelty (carryover/repeat) items on unchanged regions — not newly-surfaced ones — and rename the test case accordingly (e.g., 'unchanged-region low-priority carryover → dropped').
findings.finding: The Why section says 'drop novel low-impact findings on UNCHANGED code' and the AC-9 test label says 'unchanged-region low-priority novel → dropped', but the reused `rising_floor_drop` drops LOW-novelty (carryover) items — not high-novelty (genuinely new) ones. These two characterizations conflict: an executor cannot tell whether the gate drops (a) findings the novelty model scores as LOW novelty (repeats/carryovers) that happen to be on unchanged regions, or (b) findings the novelty model scores as HIGH novelty (genuinely new) on unchanged regions. The two behaviors require different threshold comparisons and different test fixtures.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What (region_gated_floor drop condition) / AC line 4 / AC line 9 test matrix
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'novel low-impact findings' in the Why section with 'low-novelty (carryover) low-priority findings'; update AC-9 test label to 'unchanged-region low-priority carryover (low novelty score) → dropped' to match rising_floor_drop semantics.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the `attest._hash_file` dependency explicitly and either (a) propose that `attest` exports a stable public `hash_file` function, or (b) inline a trivial sha256 helper in `region_gate.py` to avoid the private-API coupling.
findings.finding: The plan uses `attest._hash_file` (a private/internal function, underscore-prefixed) as the file-hashing primitive for region detection, crossing a module boundary into an unexported API without acknowledging or mitigating the coupling risk.
findings.location: ## What — region_gate.py section: 'hashes the current file (attest._hash_file)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line public re-export in `attest/__init__.py` (e.g., `hash_file = _hash_file`) or define `def _hash_file(path): import hashlib; return hashlib.sha256(Path(path).read_bytes()).hexdigest()` directly in `region_gate.py` to eliminate the cross-module private dependency.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the plan (Scope or a 'Caller impact' note) which callers of the `workflow_ops.py` produce path exist and confirm they are unaffected by the filter insertion, or list any that need updates.
findings.finding: The plan modifies the `workflow_ops.py` produce path by inserting a filter step but does not acknowledge downstream callers that currently receive the full (unfiltered) finding set, leaving it ambiguous whether they need updates or are backward-compatible.
findings.location: What section: 'NEW `region_gated_floor` (in `code_review/workflow_ops.py`, applied over the decided advisory set in the produce path before the sidecar emit)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the `code_review_novelty` alias is structurally necessary (e.g., if the code-review novelty sub-call needs a *different* model shape in the future) or whether `RunRequest(output_schema="novelty")` — pointing at the already-registered kernel contract — is sufficient; remove the alias if the model is byte-identical and no gate-specific field extension is planned.
findings.finding: The plan registers `novelty_model` under a third alias `code_review_novelty` (via `contracts.register_contract("code_review_novelty", novelty_model)` in `code_review/contracts.py`) despite the kernel already registering the same byte-identical model as `"novelty"` (`verify.py:446`) and plan-review aliasing it as `"plan_review_novelty"` (`passes.py:208`). The `RunRequest(output_schema="code_review_novelty")` in the novelty sub-call could reference `"novelty"` directly, as the model is domain-agnostic by design. Adding a third gate-specific alias for an identical model, purely to mirror the plan-review registration convention, is cargo-cult: copying the pattern without the structural reason that justified it for plan-review (which could hypothetically need a different novelty shape; code-review uses the same one).
findings.location: What section — 'Register the reused contract at `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`'; AC2 — '`code_review_novelty` contract is registered'
findings.suggested_fix: In `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`, omit the `code_review_novelty` registration and use `output_schema="novelty"` in the `RunRequest` for the novelty sub-call. This is the kernel's canonical name and already registered at import time. If a future code-review-specific novelty shape is anticipated, add a comment explaining the forward-compat rationale instead of registering now.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the reviewer id and file name: either rename the file to `code-novelty.md` (matching the id `code-novelty`) or change the `RunRequest(reviewers=[...])` to use `"code-review-novelty"` (matching what the underscore-to-dash normalizer derives from `code_review_novelty.md`).
findings.finding: The reviewer id `code-novelty` in `RunRequest(reviewers=["code-novelty"])` will NOT resolve to the file `code_review_novelty.md`. The `get_prompt` resolver's `_packaged_prompt_files()` derives the prompt id by replacing underscores with dashes in the file stem, so `code_review_novelty.md` → id `code-review-novelty`, not `code-novelty`. A `get_prompt("code-novelty")` call will raise `PromptNotFound`.
findings.location: What section — 'NEW code-review novelty PROMPT + reviewer id … `code-novelty` reviewer id used in the sub-call's `RunRequest(reviewers=["code-novelty"])`'
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the plan-review pattern exactly: name the file `code_review_novelty.md` (already chosen) and set `PASS_NOVELTY_CODE = "code-review-novelty"` as the reviewer id in the RunRequest, since `code_review_novelty.md` → normalized id `code-review-novelty`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `region_gated_floor` is a `@register_step` (requiring a new entry in `code-review.yaml`) or a plain function called directly from `_run_code_review_gate` in `gate_dispatch.py`; if the former, add `code-review.yaml` to the Scope.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` is not listed in the plan's Scope section, but if `region_gated_floor` is implemented as a `@register_step` in `workflow_ops.py` and must run between the `verdict` step output and the sidecar emit in `gate_dispatch.py`, it likely requires a new step in `code-review.yaml` (analogous to how plan-review's post-verdict novelty floor is wired). The YAML gate is an unenumerated edit target.
findings.location: Scope section — lists `gate_dispatch.py` for 'invoke the floor before emit' but omits `code-review.yaml`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether a `code-novelty` entry must be added to `index.json` (with `fallback_file: code_review_novelty.md`, `outputs: code_review_novelty`, `execution_mode: single_turn`) or document the exact filename-convention rule that makes `reviewers=["code-novelty"]` resolve to `code_review_novelty.md` without an index entry.
findings.finding: The `code_review_novelty.md` prompt declares `outputs: code_review_novelty`, and the plan specifies `RunRequest(reviewers=["code-novelty"], output_schema="code_review_novelty")`, but the plan never specifies how the reviewer id `code-novelty` resolves to the prompt file `code_review_novelty.md`. The reviewer index (`index.json`) does not contain `plan-novelty` (the plan-review analogue used in production at `__init__.py:161`) either, so the resolution is by convention — but that convention (id `→` fallback_file via `{id.replace('-','_')}.md`? or via a new index entry?) is never stated. Without an index.json entry for `code-novelty`, the prompt-resolution path may fail or fall back silently.
findings.location: What / NEW code-review novelty PROMPT + reviewer id; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/index.json
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `code-novelty` entry to `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/index.json` mirroring the shape of any existing single-turn sub-call reviewer, with `fallback_file: code_review_novelty.md`, `execution_mode: single_turn`, `category: code-review-pass`, and `outputs: code_review_novelty`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
